channel.title: EMERGENZ Biosecurity Intelligence Dashboard
channel.description: High-severity biosecurity signals and recent authority/media coverage. Generated 2026-07-02T09:26:31.370Z.

item.title: [ACTION] Ebola disease — Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo & Uganda
item.description: WHO has declared the Bundibugyo ebolavirus outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with cross-border spread to Uganda. As of June 11, 2026 (Brown Pandemic Center Tracking Report, sourcing WHO), the DRC has reported 635 confirmed cases and 127 confirmed deaths (CFR ~20%), across 25 affected health zones in Ituri, North Kivu, and South Kivu. Uganda has reported 19 confirmed cases (2 confirmed deaths, 1 probable death) — a total of at least 654 cases and 130 deaths across both countries. Contact-tracing compliance is above 80% in North/South Kivu but only 43% in Ituri. Nakivale refugee camp (Southwest Uganda, population ~150,000) identified 13 suspected cases, all quarantined. The regional response has escalated: Uganda border health measures, a contested US-supported quarantine/care arrangement in Kenya, a Brazilian investigation of possible imported cases, and accelerated multi-candidate vaccine efforts. These figures are updated from WHO-derived reporting and are subject to retrospective revision — verify the latest WHO situation report before operational use. U.S. Embassy Kampala/State Department health alerts are now monitored as official operational sentinels for travel and cross-border posture, but they do not overwrite WHO/CDC/MOH/Africa CDC epidemiologic counts by themselves. Current official disease tracking remains Bundibugyo Ebola; Marburg references are treated as disambiguation or staged candidates unless corroborated by primary public-health authorities.

item.title: [CONCERN] Andes virus hantavirus cluster — MV Hondius travel-linked
item.description: ECDC reports an Andes hantavirus cluster linked to the MV Hondius expedition cruise, with 13 total reported cases (12 confirmed and 1 probable) and 3 deaths as of 17 June 2026.

item.title: [CONCERN] Measles — United States
item.description: CDC confirmed 2,104 measles cases in 2026 as of June 18 — the highest US annual total since 1991 — across 41 jurisdictions and 30 outbreaks, with 93% of cases outbreak-associated. The 2026 total has surpassed the full-year 2025 total (2,074 cases). No deaths reported in 2026. U.S. kindergartener MMR coverage has declined to 92.5% (from 95.2% in 2019–2020), leaving approximately 286,000 kindergarteners unvaccinated — the structural driver of sustained outbreak activity.

item.title: [CONCERN] Mpox — clade I, Africa & travel-linked importations
item.description: WHO external situation report #65 remains the registered WHO snapshot for the multi-country mpox outbreak: global epidemiology is current through 31 March 2026, Africa through 19 April 2026, and operational response updates through 22 April 2026. Use current WHO or Africa CDC updates before changing country-level counts or importation details.

item.title: [CONCERN] Cholera — sub-Saharan Africa, including Republic of Congo
item.description: Cholera remains an active sub-Saharan Africa monitoring signal. The reviewed Africa CDC source lane is the current official outbreak/intelligence entry point; specific country and continent-wide counts should stay staged until verified against the latest Africa CDC or WHO primary report.

item.title: [CONCERN] Chikungunya — global outbreak activity
item.description: Chikungunya remains under PAHO/WHO epidemiological-alert monitoring for the Americas and other mosquito-active regions. Country-level burdens and travel notices should be refreshed only from the latest PAHO, WHO, CDC, or national public-health source before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Yellow fever — Colombia (Tolima) & the Americas
item.description: PAHO reported sustained yellow fever transmission in parts of South America, including areas beyond traditional Amazonian risk zones such as Sao Paulo state in Brazil and Tolima department in Colombia. During the first seven weeks of 2026, 34 human cases and 15 deaths were confirmed across Bolivia, Colombia, Peru, and Venezuela.

item.title: Google News: Financial innovation can deliver a Bundibugyo Ebola vaccine - Nature
item.description: Financial innovation can deliver a Bundibugyo Ebola vaccine Nature

item.title: Google News: A scientist’s warning about screwworm outbreak and politicizing research - San Antonio Express-News
item.description: A scientist’s warning about screwworm outbreak and politicizing research San Antonio Express-News

item.title: Google News: Measles Guidance for Gastroenterologists and Hepatologists - Gastroenterology & Endoscopy News
item.description: Measles Guidance for Gastroenterologists and Hepatologists Gastroenterology & Endoscopy News

item.title: NPR: Federal civil rights data holds schools accountable. Under Trump, it's 6 months late
item.description: The Education Department has long collected civil rights data about things like bullying, harassment and disability services in schools, but it hasn't made the latest information public.

item.title: Google News: Understanding Ebola’s wildlife origins is crucial to preventing next big outbreak - The Guardian
item.description: Understanding Ebola’s wildlife origins is crucial to preventing next big outbreak The Guardian

item.title: NPR: As a heatwave grips the U.S., a look at how extreme heat affects the body
item.description: As heatwaves sweep the country, NPR's A Martinez asks W. Larry Kenney, professor of physiology and kinesiology at Penn State University, how extreme heat affects humans.

item.title: Google News: GSK Korea announced on the 2nd that its respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) vaccine "AlexB" has been a.. - 매일경제
item.description: GSK Korea announced on the 2nd that its respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) vaccine "AlexB" has been a.. 매일경제

item.title: UKHSA: High consequence infectious disease: country specific risk
item.description: Listing of countries with a known occurrence of high consequence infectious disease (HCID).

item.title: UKHSA: Research: Environmental monitoring following the Grenfell Tower fire
item.description: Air quality reports for the survivors and residents of the area surrounding Grenfell Tower.

item.title: UKHSA: Research: Sexually transmitted infections: London data
item.description: Reports presenting data since 2014 for sexually transmitted infections (STIs) in London.

item.title: UKHSA: Official Statistics: Respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) maternal vaccination coverage in England
item.description: Official statistics data and reporting on vaccine uptake for the RSV (respiratory syncytial virus) vaccination programme for pregnant women.

item.title: UKHSA: Official Statistics: National norovirus and rotavirus surveillance reports: 2025 to 2026 season
item.description: Reports of norovirus and rotavirus laboratory surveillance and enteric virus outbreaks in hospital settings in England during the 2025 to 2026 season.

item.title: UKHSA: Official Statistics: Respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) older adults vaccination coverage in England
item.description: Vaccine uptake data for the respiratory syncytial virus (RSV) vaccination programme for older adults.

item.title: UKHSA: Guidance: Pre-release access lists: National norovirus and rotavirus surveillance reports
item.description: Lists of those receiving pre-release access to UK Health Security Agency national norovirus and rotavirus surveillance reports

item.title: STAT News: STAT+: A ‘historic’ FDA clearance raises the question: Is LLM the interface? Or the decision-maker?
item.description: The newly cleared device is based around an app that helps a patient manage their diabetes using a treatment plan defined by their doctor.

item.title: STAT News: The new presidential fitness test is a positive step, not the solution, experts say
item.description: Physical activity needs to be fun to keep kids engaged. And the test risks embarrassing them, instead.

item.title: STAT News: STAT+: A year after distressed buyout, what’s become of Bluebird Bio?
item.description: Bluebird Bio was a dark biotech morality tale when David Meek bought it. He says the new company, Genetix, is already making money.

item.title: STAT News: STAT+: A former AI regulator, now in industry, says biopharma is reading FDA’s guidance wrong
item.description: Companies are being too conservative in how they interpret FDA's AI guidance, but the agency can do more to help, too, Tala Fakhouri says.

item.title: STAT News: Opinion: CDC leadership continues to try manufacture a scientific debate on vaccines where none exists
item.description: CDC leadership continues to try manufacture a scientific debate on vaccines where none exists, writes Ben Lopman.

item.title: STAT News: Opinion: Teens are turning to chatbots for mental health help. We need rules to keep them safe
item.description: The share of young people using AI chatbots for mental health advice rose more than 40% in a single year, researcher writes.
